nativeRange.habitat: Marine shallow-reef and coastal environments; typically found in the photic zone on hard substrate

avoidWith: herbivorous fish that may graze macroalgae (e.g., large tangs, rabbitfish)

imageCandidates.notes: Primary product image for Finger Nemastoma listing
Pink Thorny Nemastoma variant product image
Rose Petal Nemastoma variant product image

summary: A gelatinous, finger-like red macroalgae (family Nemastomataceae) sold in several color forms — red, orange-tipped, pink, and rose — that tend to do well in established reef displays and refugiums. Intermediate care due to delicate texture and holdfast difficulty.
careNotes:
Nemastoma sp. is a specialty red macroalgae in the family Nemastomataceae (order Nemastomatales, class Florideophyceae, phylum Rhodophyta). It has been documented in four distinct trade color forms: the base red 'Finger Nemastoma', the 'Orange-Tipped Nemastoma' (fluorescent orange tips visible under actinic lighting), the 'Pink Thorny Nemastoma', and the 'Rose Petal Nemastoma'. All four share identical husbandry requirements and the same gelatinous, slippery thallus texture.

This species is sold exclusively by Mosaic Macros in the hobby; no AlgaeBarn, Top Shelf Aquatics, or other major retailer listings were found. Family-level husbandry extrapolation applies.

Anchoring: The thallus is notably thin, gelatinous, and slippery — it should NOT be secured with cyanoacrylate glue (super glue). Mosaic recommends tying down with fishing line or loosely anchoring with a clear zip-tie until the specimen develops a holdfast. Holdfast formation tends to be slow and unreliable; patience and minimal disturbance are advised.

Shipping sensitivity: All four Mosaic listings require UPS Next-Day Air (or 2-Day Express minimum) for live arrival guarantee coverage, indicating this species is more sensitive to transport stress than typical macroalgae.

Reef compatibility: Generally considered safe with standard reef livestock; has been documented serving dual functions as a nutrient-exporting macroalgae and as microhabitat for copepods and amphipods. Research specific herbivorous fish compatibility before adding to tanks with heavy grazers.

Water parameters follow standard reef values. No specific published data for this species was available beyond Mosaic product descriptions; parameters listed reflect standard saltwater reef conventions applicable to ornamental red macroalgae in the Nemastomatales. Per Mosaic care guide guidance: as a red or brown alga, this species will MELT at the base if buried in sand — attach to rock via superglue, zip-tie/fishing line, or wedging instead. (Sand-bed acceptance per Mosaic is limited to Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea genera, plus some calcified Dasycladales.)
breedingNotes: Propagation by fragmentation has been reported to be difficult in practice due to the gelatinous texture and poor holdfast formation from cuttings. The family Nemastomataceae undergoes alternation of generations (gametophyte-sporophyte), but sexual reproduction has not been documented in aquarium conditions.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby-trade source. Four SKUs cross-verified: Finger Nemastoma, Orange-Tipped Nemastoma, Pink Thorny Nemastoma, Rose Petal Nemastoma. All confirm gelatinous texture, holdfast difficulty, shipping sensitivity, and intermediate care level.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Orange-Tipped Nemastoma
additional.notes: Cross-verified: same care notes, fluorescent orange tips under actinic lighting noted as distinguishing visual trait.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Pink Thorny Nemastoma
additional.notes: Cross-verified: confirms gelatinous texture, no-glue anchoring requirement, and advanced-beginner care level.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Rose Petal Nemastoma
additional.notes: Cross-verified: confirms identical care notes across all four color variants.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Nemastomatales
additional.notes: Taxonomy source: confirms Order Nemastomatales, Family Nemastomataceae, Class Florideophyceae (Rhodophyta). AlgaeBase was CAPTCHA-blocked during research session.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
